Scenario.title: Operation Spalax: Cyber Espionage Against Healthcare Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex A skilled threat group named Operation Spalax executed a multi-stage cyber attack targeting healthcare organizations, leveraging spearphishing, credential theft, and lateral movement to exfiltrate sensitive medical data and disrupt critical systems.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Intellectual property theft and disruption of healthcare operations

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Social engineering", "Internet-facing assets", "Vulnerability", "Shared DB or directory"]

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: Adversaries gathered information about healthcare staff and infrastructure via public databases and social engineering to identify potential targets for exploitation.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: A fake healthcare vendor website was created to host malicious payloads, serving as a staging ground for initial access attempts.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: A malicious .docm file was delivered via spearphishing email, exploiting the recipient's trust to install malware on their endpoint.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The exploit leveraged unpatched vulnerabilities in legacy medical devices or EMR systems to execute arbitrary code remotely.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Malicious scheduled tasks were created using Windows Task Scheduler to maintain persistent access across system reboots.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Hardcoded credentials in healthcare-specific applications like PACS systems were exploited to escalate privileges and access restricted areas.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malware injected itself into legitimate healthcare software processes (e.g., MATLAB) to evade detection by security tools.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Credentials were extracted from memory using Mimikatz, enabling further lateral movement across the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: PowerShell scripts were used to map internal network topology and identify systems containing PHI or research data.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Stolen credentials were used to move laterally between hospital departments via RDP sessions.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Prioritized exfiltration of PHI, clinical trial data, and vaccine research from centralized repositories.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data was transferred via HTTPS and DNS tunneling to avoid detection by standard firewall/IPS rules.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Ransomware encrypted critical systems and deleted backups, disrupting healthcare operations and demanding payment for decryption.

Scenario.summary: Operation Spalax executed a sophisticated, multi-stage attack on healthcare institutions, beginning with spearphishing and escalating to ransomware deployment. The campaign exploited both technical vulnerabilities and human factors to achieve persistence, lateral movement, and data exfiltration, ultimately crippling critical healthcare systems.
